message:
fix(scheduler): rewrite timer ID allocation with uniform wrap-around and exhaustion detection

The previous allocate_timer_id() had two disjoint code paths: a fast
path for the common case (next_timer_id < MAX) and a linear scan
fallback from 1..u64::MAX when the counter overflows. The linear scan
could be extremely slow if many IDs were in use and the first free ID
was at a high offset.

Rewrite as a single loop that walks forward from next_timer_id,
wrapping from u64::MAX back to 1, and checks each candidate against
timer_id_in_use(). Exhaustion is detected by checking if the candidate
loops back to the starting position, making the worst case O(n) where
n = number of active timers rather than O(u64::MAX).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
